about.label: ABOUT DANIEL
about.headline: CREATIVE DEVELOPER IN MOTION
about.body: I build small visual systems that connect motion graphics, interactive code, and early ML/data experiments. My work is still growing, but it is grounded in curiosity, clear process, and a love for turning abstract ideas into moving, usable things.

projects.name: SIGNAL MOTION LAB
projects.description: A small creative coding prototype that turns simple data signals into motion-led visual states. Built as a bridge between my motion design background and my newer data/ML practice.
projects.objective: Show how a simple dataset can become a visual motion system instead of a static chart.
projects.approach: Map cleaned signal values to animation states, timing, scale and color so the motion language carries the data.
projects.outcome: A small hybrid prototype that explains the direction of my creative developer practice.
projects.nextStep: Turn the prototype into a live demo with user-uploaded CSV data and a short technical writeup.

projects.name: CHROME FLOW
projects.description: Experimental fluid simulation using high-viscosity liquid metal. Exploring the interplay between light and motion through procedural generation techniques.
projects.objective: Create a polished motion study around liquid metal, reflection and slow cinematic pacing.
projects.approach: Use 3D simulation, lighting and post-production to explore a single material system deeply.
projects.outcome: A focused motion piece that demonstrates visual taste, material direction and timing.
projects.nextStep: Add breakdown frames showing the simulation setup, lighting decisions and compositing passes.

projects.name: SILVER SHIFT
projects.description: A study on light dispersion and brushed aluminum reflections. Combining real-time rendering with post-production grading.
projects.objective: Explore how metallic surfaces and dispersion can create a quiet, premium visual identity.
projects.approach: Build a compact rendering study, then refine contrast, grading and reflection rhythm in post.
projects.outcome: A motion-focused visual study that keeps the Behance-style image sequence as the main evidence.
projects.nextStep: Add before/after frames and a short note about render and grading decisions.

projects.name: PORTFOLIO ADMIN SYSTEM
projects.description: A practical admin workflow for managing portfolio content, uploads, messages, and project metadata. It is a small real-world development project focused on making the site easier to maintain.
projects.objective: Make the portfolio easier to maintain without editing source files for every content update.
projects.approach: Use Next.js server actions, typed project metadata and an admin workflow for content, uploads and messages.
projects.outcome: A practical site-management prototype that proves frontend and product workflow thinking.
projects.nextStep: Replace placeholder links with the real repository and add screenshots of the admin flow.

projects.name: DARK MERCURY
projects.description: Minimalist motion study in a low-gravity vacuum environment. Abstract forms that challenge perception of space and time.
projects.objective: Build an abstract low-gravity scene with restraint, atmosphere and spatial tension.
projects.approach: Focus on minimal forms, controlled camera movement and soft contrast instead of visual overload.
projects.outcome: A cinematic motion study that shows pacing and art-direction judgment.
projects.nextStep: Add a short process note on references, camera movement and lighting constraints.

projects.name: VOID GEOMETRY
projects.description: Procedural generation of monolithic architectural structures. Where mathematics meets cinematic visual storytelling.
projects.objective: Connect procedural generation with cinematic architectural composition.
projects.approach: Generate monolithic forms, then treat the output like a designed scene rather than a raw procedural render.
projects.outcome: A generative motion project that sits naturally between technical systems and visual storytelling.
projects.nextStep: Document the procedural rules and show a few alternative outputs.

projects.name: LEARNING DATA NOTEBOOK
projects.description: A compact data science learning project for cleaning a small dataset, exploring patterns, and presenting the result visually. The focus is process, interpretation, and honest iteration.
projects.objective: Practice the full beginner data workflow: clean, explore, interpret and present a small dataset.
projects.approach: Use Python and notebooks to inspect patterns, then translate the result into a concise visual explanation.
projects.outcome: A modest but honest data project that shows learning process and interpretation.
projects.nextStep: Publish the notebook, add charts and write a one-page case study about what changed after cleaning.
